response:
for each element in the array nums we need to compute a value based on its position and the other elements in the array if the index is not a multiple of three we calculate the sum of absolute differences between nums[i] and all other elements if the index is a multiple of three we calculate the sum of squares of the differences between nums[i] and all other elements the array is sorted in non-decreasing order which allows us to optimize the calculation using prefix sums for the absolute differences part we can use the prefix sum array to quickly compute the sum of absolute differences for each element this reduces the time complexity from o(n^2) to o(n) for the square differences part we can directly compute the sum for each element by iterating through the array and summing the squares of the differences

***

the approach involves first computing the prefix sum array to help with the absolute differences calculation for each index i we can split the array into elements before i and after i using the prefix sum array we can quickly find the sum of elements before and after i and use that to compute the total absolute difference for that index for the square differences part we iterate through the array and for each index that is a multiple of three we calculate the sum of (nums[i] - nums[j])^2 for all j in the array this ensures that the solution is efficient and meets the constraints of the problem the final result array is built by combining the computed values for each index based on whether it is a multiple of three or not
